title: Sparkwright Host Message
description: Wire envelope for all messages exchanged between a Sparkwright host (server-runtime + transport) and a client (TUI, web SDK, etc). Newline-delimited JSON over stdio or one message per WebSocket frame. Every message is one of: request (client→host, expects response), response (host→client, paired with a request by id), or event (host→client, fire-and-forget). The host protocol is versioned via the top-level `protocolVersion` field on the handshake; v1.x is backwards compatible (fields may be added but never removed or repurposed). See docs/HOST_PROTOCOL.md.

MessageId.description: Stable identifier the sender chose. For requests, the response echoes the same id.

Timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp at which the sender produced the message.

protocolVersion.description: Client's spoken protocol version (major.minor). Host rejects with protocol_version_mismatch when major differs from its own.

capabilities.description: Optional list of optional features the client supports. Hosts ignore unknown entries.

controlSessionId.description: Parent/control session attribution for a workflow job; it must not be used as the job session storage identity.

targetPath.description: Workspace-relative target path that the run should focus on when applicable.

confidentialPaths.description: Additional workspace-relative paths/globs whose contents this run must not read.

confidentialDefaults.description: Whether built-in conservative confidential path defaults are included. Defaults to true.

shouldWrite.description: Whether this run is allowed to request workspace writes.

workflow.description: Workflow asset name to instantiate for this run.

accessMode.description: High-level run autonomy preset. When present, the host compiles it to permissionMode + shouldWrite and ignores a conflicting legacy permissionMode.

runId.description: Prior run to resume from a persisted checkpoint or reconstructed trace.

sessionId.description: Optional session scope used to disambiguate where the prior run lives.

targetPath.description: Workspace-relative target path that the resumed run should focus on when applicable.

confidentialPaths.description: Additional workspace-relative paths/globs whose contents this run must not read.

confidentialDefaults.description: Whether built-in conservative confidential path defaults are included. Defaults to true.

shouldWrite.description: Whether the resumed run is allowed to request workspace writes.

fromTrace.description: Reconstruct a best-effort checkpoint from trace.jsonl when checkpoint.json is absent.

force.description: Allow resuming checkpoints that are terminal or otherwise normally refused.

accessMode.description: High-level run autonomy preset. When present, the host compiles it to permissionMode + shouldWrite and ignores a conflicting legacy permissionMode.

compaction.description: Include a compact.json/session compaction event audit view without compacted summary content.

reason.description: Optional caller label recorded in compaction diagnostics.

sessionId.description: Optional session scope used to limit workflow-run lookup.

workflowRunId.description: Durable workflow instance id to resume.

sessionId.description: Optional session scope used to disambiguate where the workflow run lives.

targetPath.description: Workspace-relative target path that the resumed run should focus on when applicable.

confidentialPaths.description: Additional workspace-relative paths/globs whose contents this run must not read.

confidentialDefaults.description: Whether built-in conservative confidential path defaults are included. Defaults to true.

shouldWrite.description: Whether the resumed run is allowed to request workspace writes.

accessMode.description: High-level run autonomy preset. When present, the host compiles it to permissionMode + shouldWrite and ignores a conflicting legacy permissionMode.

sessionId.description: Reserved for future scoped inspection. Omit to inspect this connection's current host capability state.

model.description: Model reference in provider/model form, or the reserved deterministic model.

shouldWrite.description: Whether this inspection should describe workspace-write-capable runs.

accessMode.description: High-level run autonomy preset used to scope diagnostics. Preferred over permissionMode and shouldWrite when present.

backgroundTasks.description: Session-level foreground/background task policy used to scope diagnostics.

permissionMode.description: Legacy approval mode used when accessMode is absent.

protocolVersion.description: Host's spoken protocol version. Always sent immediately after a successful handshake.

event.description: A SparkwrightEvent as defined by schemas/event.schema.json. Passed through verbatim; clients can re-validate against that schema if they need to.
